expectations.text: Correctly states that GitHub does NOT support importing secrets (only overwrite-destination is available)
expectations.evidence: Does not explicitly state that GitHub doesn't support importing secrets. Says 'Infisical's GitHub integration typically only manages secrets it creates' but doesn't clearly indicate GitHub API limitations

expectations.text: Recommends backing up or manually exporting existing GitHub secrets before syncing
expectations.evidence: "Before syncing: Document your existing GitHub secrets. Go to your GitHub repo → Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions. Take screenshots or export a list of all current secrets"

expectations.text: Mentions repository scope option for GitHub sync destination
expectations.evidence: "Select Your Repository: Choose the GitHub repository where you want secrets synced"
References 'GitHub integration' and 'Create Integration' but does not use the Infisical-specific term 'App Connection'

expectations.text: Recommends Key Schema or Disable Secret Deletion to protect manually-managed secrets
expectations.evidence: Mentions naming conventions and non-conflicting names, but does not recommend Key Schema template or 'Disable Secret Deletion' feature

expectations.text: Warns that existing GitHub secrets not in Infisical may be overwritten
expectations.evidence: "Before syncing: Document your existing GitHub secrets...Sync Only New Secrets...Verify No Overwrites: Confirm with Infisical's documentation"
